name: SVG vs PNG vs WebP vs Lottie vs Rive vs MP4 — Decision Framework
definition_origin: Asset format choice is the structural decision that determines file size, runtime cost, animation capability, accessibility, and whether the asset can be themed or interacted with at runtime. Each format exists because of a specific physics tradeoff: vector formats (SVG, Lottie, Rive) describe shapes mathematically and scale infinitely; raster formats (PNG, WebP) bake pixels and excel at photographic complexity; video formats (MP4, WebM) encode frame-by-frame motion with high compression but low interactivity.
core_purpose: Format choice maps directly to Don Norman's tripartite levels. Visceral: a chunky animated mascot in Rive or Lottie creates instant emotional impact (Duolingo's bounce, Headspace's breathing circle). Behavioral: SVG icons must respond to interaction state (hover, press, disabled) without jank — Rive state machines or CSS-animated SVGs do this best. Reflective: brand-identity assets (logos, illustrations) benefit from SVG's crispness on retina, or Lottie's repeatable brand motion that users associate with the product. The wrong format breaks the level — a giant MP4 onboarding screen kills visceral delight via load delay, and a JPG icon kills reflective brand quality at 3x density.
when_needed: Consumer mobile (Kleeve, fitness, meditation, language): heavy on Rive/Lottie for emotional state moments + SVG for icons + WebP for photo assets. B2B/dev-tool: SVG-dominant (icons, charts, status indicators) + minimal Lottie for empty states. AI-wrapper (chat-style): SVG icons + occasional Lottie for loading/thinking states. Marketing surfaces (landing, app store): WebP hero photos + MP4/WebM for app preview videos + SVG for logo/badges.
format_options:
DECISION MATRIX BY ASSET TYPE:

| Asset Type | Best Format | Fallback | Avoid |
|---|---|---|---|
| Icons (UI) | SVG | PNG @3x | MP4, Lottie (overkill) |
| Logo | SVG | PNG @3x | JPG (transparency lost) |
| Photographic hero | WebP | JPG | PNG (5-10x larger), SVG |
| Photo with transparency | WebP | PNG | JPG |
| Linear character animation | Lottie/dotLottie | Animated WebP | MP4 (10x larger) |
| Interactive character | Rive | Lottie + JS | MP4 |
| State-driven UI motion (toggle, FAB) | Rive | CSS-animated SVG | Lottie (no native interactivity) |
| Onboarding empty-state animation | Lottie/dotLottie | MP4 | GIF (deprecated) |
| App preview video (store/landing) | MP4 (H.264) + WebM (VP9) | — | Lottie (cannot do photo motion) |
| Loading spinner | SVG + CSS or Rive | Lottie | MP4 |
| Mascot reactive to scroll/gesture | Rive | — | Lottie (limited interactivity) |
| Decorative gradient blob | SVG | PNG | Lottie (overkill) |
| Complex 3D illustration | PNG/WebP | — | SVG (path-explosion), Lottie |

FORMAT-BY-FORMAT PROS/CONS:

SVG: vector, infinite scale, animatable via CSS/SMIL/JS/GSAP, smallest for geometric assets, themeable via currentColor, screen-reader friendly with role=img + title + desc. Cons: blow-up file size on photo-realistic illustrations (every bezier point counts); SMIL deprecated on web (use CSS/JS); iOS Safari has historic clipping bugs.

PNG: lossless raster, alpha channel, universal support. Cons: 5-10x larger than WebP at equivalent quality; no scale beyond bake resolution; need @1x/@2x/@3x ladders for mobile.

WebP: 25-50% smaller than JPEG, 26% smaller than PNG, supports lossy + lossless + alpha + animation. Browser support universal in 2026 (Safari since macOS Big Sur 2020 / iOS 14). React Native: bundled-asset support iOS only by default; Android needs Fresco config in build.gradle, or use react-native-webp-format / Expo Image which handles WebP transparently.

Lottie (.json) / dotLottie (.lottie): vector animation exported from After Effects (Bodymovin) or Lottielab. Linear playback, scriptable speed/segments. dotLottie is gzipped ZIP container — 50-80% smaller than raw JSON, bundles assets. lottie-react-native 7.3.6 (npm, 2026). Cons: not designed for runtime interactivity (state machines added in 2024 are limited); CPU-bound SVG renderer can drop frames; benchmarks show ~17fps in RN under load.

Rive (.riv): binary vector + state machines + data binding. GPU-accelerated via Metal (iOS) / WebGL (web) / Vulkan (Android). 10-15x smaller than equivalent Lottie (e.g. 240KB Lottie ≈ 16KB Rive). Hits ~60fps in RN benchmarks where Lottie hits ~17fps. State machines wire idle→hover→pressed→loading transitions visually. February 2026: Rive acquired by Canva, made fully free for individuals including commercial use [uncertain]. Runtime: @rive-app/react-native (requires react-native-nitro-modules).

MP4 (H.264) / WebM (VP9): heaviest, no transparency on most platforms (HEVC w/ alpha is iOS-only), best for true video content (app previews, real footage). 10x larger than equivalent Lottie for vector animation. Use only when content is genuinely photographic motion.
file_size_performance: TARGET FILE SIZES (mobile-first): SVG icon <4KB; SVG illustration <30KB; PNG @3x icon <20KB; WebP hero <80KB; WebP @1080p hero <200KB; Lottie JSON <100KB (target <50KB); dotLottie 50-80% smaller than equivalent JSON; Rive .riv <30KB for icons, <150KB for character; MP4 app preview <3MB for 15s. Optimization: SVGO for SVG (target 30-60% reduction); cwebp -q 80 for WebP; LottieFiles Optimizer / TinyLottie / Lottiemizer for Lottie (30-70% typical); ffmpeg with H.264 high profile + 2-pass for MP4. Runtime cost: Lottie is CPU-bound (jank risk with 3+ concurrent); Rive is GPU-bound (60fps with many objects); SVG with CSS transforms is GPU-composited; MP4 is hardware-decoded but heavy on memory.
free_tools: 1) **SVGO** (svgomg.net web UI / npm) — free, removes redundant XML, target 30-60% size reduction. 2) **Squoosh.app** (Google, free, web) — best WebP/AVIF/PNG converter in-browser, no upload to server. 3) **LottieFiles Optimizer** (lottiefiles.com/optimize) — free web tool, smart keyframe + image compression. 4) **TinyLottie** (tiny-lottie.vercel.app) — free Lottie/dotLottie optimizer. 5) **Lottiemizer** (lottiemizer.com) — free, focused on shape simplification. 6) **Lottielab** (lottielab.com) — free tier web-based Lottie editor (no After Effects needed). 7) **Rive editor** — free for individuals (post-Canva acquisition Feb 2026 [uncertain]) including commercial. 8) **HandBrake** — free MP4/WebM transcoder, GUI for ffmpeg. 9) **ffmpeg** (CLI) — free, scriptable, best-in-class for MP4/WebM. 10) **ImageMagick** — free CLI for batch raster conversion.
paid_tools: Starter $10-20/mo: **Adobe Creative Cloud Photography** (~$10/mo) for Photoshop export pipelines if needed. Pro $20-30/mo: **Adobe Single-App After Effects** (~$23/mo) — only justified if you need pro motion design with Bodymovin → Lottie. Pro $20-30/mo: **LottieFiles Pro** (~$20/mo) — collaboration, larger file storage, advanced optimizer. Enterprise $50+/mo: **Rive Voyager** ($32/mo) was the previous tier; post-Canva acquisition the free tier covers individuals so paid only justified for studios/teams [uncertain]. For a solo broke dev, none of these are needed — free tools cover 95% of the workflow.
gemini_pro_workflow: Format choice itself is not generated by Gemini, but Gemini Pro accelerates the upstream asset creation that feeds each format. WORKFLOW: 1) Use **Imagen 4** in Gemini app to generate initial illustration concept (PNG output). 2) For SVG conversion: feed the PNG into a vectorizer (Recraft AI free tier, vectorizer.ai trial, or Inkscape Trace Bitmap) — Imagen 4 cannot output SVG natively. 3) For Lottie: prompt Imagen 4 for static keyframes, then assemble in Lottielab or AE. 4) For Rive: generate static character art in Imagen 4, import to Rive, build state machine manually. 5) For WebP hero: generate in Imagen 4, run through Squoosh. 6) Use **Gems** to build a 'Format Decision Gem' loaded with this matrix as a knowledge file — paste asset description, Gem returns recommended format + target size + tool chain. PROMPT PATTERN for Gem: 'Given asset description {X}, target platform {mobile/web}, interactivity needs {none/hover/state-driven}, recommend format with file-size budget and toolchain.' Use **NotebookLM** to load this research file + Lottie/Rive runtime docs as sources, then ask cross-format questions to make decisions for new assets.
workflow_steps: 1) Identify asset type and interactivity requirement (5 min) — is it static, linear-animated, or state-driven? 2) Apply decision matrix above to pick primary format (5 min). 3) Set file-size budget based on platform (mobile: tight; marketing web: looser) (2 min). 4) Generate or source the asset (Imagen 4 for raster, Lottielab/Rive for animated, Iconify for icons) (15-60 min). 5) Convert to chosen format if needed (PNG → SVG via vectorizer, JPG → WebP via Squoosh) (10 min). 6) Optimize: SVGO for SVG, Squoosh -q 80 for WebP, LottieFiles Optimizer for Lottie, no-op for Rive (already binary) (5 min). 7) Generate fallback for older runtimes if needed (PNG fallback for WebP on edge cases) (5 min). 8) Test on actual device — measure load time, memory, frame rate (Flipper / Chrome DevTools / Xcode Instruments) (15 min). 9) Add accessibility metadata (alt text, role=img, aria-hidden, prefers-reduced-motion fallback) (5 min). 10) Drop into codebase per claude_code_integration patterns (5 min). Total: ~1-2 hours per asset for a solo dev.
ai_prompt_templates: 1) **Format-decision Gem prompt**: 'I am building {asset_purpose} for a {consumer_mobile|B2B|AI-wrapper} app. The asset needs to {static|linear-animate|respond-to-state}. Target platform {iOS RN|web|Expo}. Recommend format from [SVG, PNG, WebP, Lottie, dotLottie, Rive, MP4] with file-size budget, runtime library, and 3 anti-slop guardrails.' 2) **Imagen 4 illustration → vectorize prompt**: 'A flat-design vector illustration of {subject}, clean geometric shapes, 5-color limited palette {hex_list}, no gradients except simple linear, transparent-friendly background, suitable for SVG vectorization.' 3) **Imagen 4 animated keyframe set**: 'Three keyframes of {character} doing {action}: pose 1 = {state}, pose 2 = {state}, pose 3 = {state}. Identical character proportions and lighting in all three. Flat design, 5-color palette {hex_list}, white background.' 4) **MP4 app-preview prompt for Veo**: 'A 6-second 1080p portrait video of an iPhone screen showing {app_name} {feature}, hand visible only at start, smooth thumb tap, no text overlays, soft natural studio lighting.'
claude_code_integration: Drop locations: SVGs in `apps/mobile/src/assets/icons/` consumed via SvgUri or react-native-svg-transformer (preferred — import as React component). Lotties in `apps/mobile/src/assets/lottie/`, loaded via lottie-react-native LottieView. Rive files in `apps/mobile/src/assets/rive/`, loaded via @rive-app/react-native Rive component. WebP/PNG in `apps/mobile/src/assets/images/`, served by Expo Image (handles WebP cross-platform). Install commands: `pnpm add react-native-svg react-native-svg-transformer lottie-react-native @rive-app/react-native react-native-nitro-modules expo-image`. Optimization scripts: add `scripts/optimize-assets.ts` running SVGO + cwebp + LottieFiles CLI in pre-commit hook. Claude Code can scaffold a `<Asset>` wrapper component that picks format per platform (Expo Image for web, native SVG/Lottie/Rive on mobile).
emotional_design_integration: Caregiver archetype: prefers Lottie/dotLottie for soft linear motion (Headspace breathing circle, Calm gentle wave). Avoid Rive's snappy state machines — feels too gamified. Magician archetype: Rive shines — interactive transformative motion (a UI element morphing between states like Linear's superhuman shortcuts, or Vercel's deploy animation). SVG with CSS for crisp brand-tier reflective polish. Jester archetype: Rive + Lottie combined — bouncy character animations (Duolingo's owl). MP4 video assets for social-share moments. Hero archetype: WebP photographic hero + MP4 montage — high-stakes visual punch. Sage archetype: minimal motion — static SVGs and crisp WebP hero, Lottie reserved for empty states only.
specific_examples: 1) **Linear (linear.app)** — uses Rive for the cycle-status interactive icon and SVG for everything else. Pure SVG icon set, no raster. Demonstrates how a B2B tool can ship without a single Lottie file. https://linear.app 2) **Duolingo** — Lottie heavy for character animations (Duo the owl celebrating, dancing) + SVG for UI icons. Their 'shape language' guide says use the fewest details needed. https://blog.duolingo.com/shape-language-duolingos-art-style/ 3) **Headspace** — Lottie for breathing animations and meditation timer; WebP for hero photography. Soft, slow Lottie loops align with Caregiver brand. https://dribbble.com/headspace 4) **Vercel (vercel.com)** — pure SVG + CSS animations for the Sage-magician dashboard aesthetic; MP4 for marketing video on landing page. 5) **Stripe** — SVG-dominant, Lottie for empty states only (e.g. 'no transactions yet'). 6) **Airbnb** — invented Lottie originally; uses Lottie for rebooking confirmation and progress states.
anti_ai_slop_traps: Format-specific slop tells: (a) **SVG slop**: 50,000-point bezier paths from auto-tracing a complex Imagen 4 photo — looks lumpy and weighs 800KB. Counter: keep illustration source flat-design with hard edges, vectorize manually in Recraft or Inkscape with simplification, target <50 nodes per shape. (b) **Lottie slop**: 'gradient-blob loading spinners' that all look the same because they came from LottieFiles' free trending page. Counter: build one custom Lottie in Lottielab matching your brand — even a hand-drawn squiggle is more distinctive. (c) **Rive slop**: default Rive demo characters (the bear, the cube) shipped without customization. Counter: always retexture/recolor to brand palette, never ship the demo asset. (d) **WebP slop**: AI-generated 'isometric people working at laptops' Imagen 4 outputs that scream stock-AI. Counter: brief Imagen 4 with specific brand archetype + lighting + ethnicity diversity + non-isometric perspective. (e) **MP4 slop**: Veo-generated app-preview videos with melted-button UI mockups. Counter: only use Veo for B-roll/atmosphere, screen-record real app for the actual product preview. EXPLICIT NEGATIVE PROMPT: 'no isometric, no gradient blobs, no melted hands, no chibi, no generic SaaS purple-blue gradient, no random floating geometric shapes, no 3D voxel render.'
common_pitfalls: 1) Shipping all formats at once: solo devs over-ship (PNG @1x@2x@3x AND WebP AND SVG) — pick one with one fallback. 2) Lottie as default for everything: 80% of motion needs are simple CSS animations on SVG. Lottie is overkill for a hover state. 3) Not testing on low-end Android: that 200KB Lottie animation hits 12fps on a Samsung A20. 4) MP4 for animation (because it's familiar): always 5-10x larger than equivalent Lottie/Rive. 5) Forgetting prefers-reduced-motion: animations must have a static fallback. 6) Inconsistent visual language across formats: SVG icons in one style, Lottie in another, Rive in a third — feels like 3 apps. 7) Embedding huge raster images inside Lottie JSON (kills the size advantage) — externalize or use dotLottie container.
accessibility_notes: SVG: role='img' + <title> + <desc> elements; aria-labelledby referencing those IDs; aria-hidden='true' for decorative-only. WebP/PNG: standard alt text. Lottie: provide aria-label on container, set autoPlay=false + respect prefers-reduced-motion (LottieView speed=0 or static frame). Rive: aria-label container, support a static-image fallback for prefers-reduced-motion; some interactive Rive elements need keyboard focus + Enter/Space activation. MP4: caption track + aria-label + autoPlay only with muted, never autoplay if user prefers-reduced-motion.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile (RN/Expo): SVG via react-native-svg, Lottie via lottie-react-native@7.3.6, Rive via @rive-app/react-native (Nitro), WebP via Expo Image (handles iOS/Android cross-platform), MP4 via react-native-video or expo-av. Need to ship asset bundles in app or use OTA. Web: SVG inline (best perf) or via ; Lottie via @lottiefiles/dotlottie-react or lottie-web; Rive via @rive-app/canvas; WebP via with PNG fallback (rarely needed in 2026); MP4 via
sources: 1) [Callstack: Lottie vs Rive Mobile App Animation](https://www.callstack.com/blog/lottie-vs-rive-optimizing-mobile-app-animation) — RN benchmark numbers. 2) [Unicorn Icons: Rive vs Lottie 2026](https://unicornicons.com/learn/rive-vs-lottie). 3) [Rive Pricing](https://rive.app/pricing). 4) [Rive blog: Rive as a Lottie Alternative](https://rive.app/blog/rive-as-a-lottie-alternative). 5) [LottieFiles Optimize Guide](https://lottiefiles.com/blog/working-with-lottie-animations/optimize-lottie-files-for-faster-page-load-speeds). 6) [dotLottie introduction](https://dotlottie.io/intro/). 7) [React Native Image docs](https://reactnative.dev/docs/image) — WebP/PNG support. 8) [Expo Image](https://docs.expo.dev/versions/latest/sdk/image/) — cross-platform WebP. 9) [SVG Accessibility — TPGi](https://www.tpgi.com/using-aria-enhance-svg-accessibility/). 10) [Duolingo shape language](https://blog.duolingo.com/shape-language-duolingos-art-style/).
